texto: They used to tell me if you’re depressed anyway, why not be depressed and take a walk instead of being depressed and staying in bed? If it makes no difference, why not get up and go out?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Rebecca Rogers Maher que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “They used to tell me if you’re depressed anyway, why not be depressed and take a walk instead of …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, depression, depressed, com foco em reflexao.

frase_original: They used to tell me if you’re depressed anyway, why not be depressed and take a walk instead of being depressed and staying in bed? If it makes no difference, why not get up and go out?

texto: But that’s depression, isn’t it? It makes you selfish. It…it shrinks your world down. Like you’re inside a tornado or something. How are you supposed to care about the things outside that storm? When it’s all you can do to just survive it?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rebecca Rogers Maher: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “But that’s depression, isn’t it? It makes you selfish.
